title: pilosella macrotricha
intro: Pilosella macrotricha is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is also known as the woolly hawkweed due to its soft, woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Pilosella macrotricha can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
